literal: the I-maker, ego-fabricator; from aham 'I' + √kṛ 'to make'
invariant_definition: The 'I-making' faculty — the cognitive function that fabricates the sense of being a particular bounded agent. In Sāṅkhya it is the third *tattva* in the evolutionary sequence (after *mahat/buddhi*); in Vedānta-psychology it is one of the four functions of the *antaḥkaraṇa* (with *manas*, *buddhi*, *citta*). All Vedāntic schools agree that *ahaṅkāra* is what falsely (or limitingly) identifies the *ātman* with the body-mind complex; its dis-identification is essential to *mokṣa*. They differ on whether *ahaṅkāra* is *vyāvahārika* and ultimately illusory (Advaita) or a real *prakṛti*-constituent that the *jīva* is genuinely associated with (Viśiṣṭādvaita, Dvaita, Vaiṣṇava bhedābheda).

per_school.definition: *Ahaṅkāra* is the principal vehicle of *adhyāsa*: it is what superimposes *anātman*-features on *ātman*. With the rise of *brahma-jñāna*, *ahaṅkāra* is dissolved (in the *jīvanmukta*'s case, *prārabdha*-residue maintains a non-binding apparent *ahaṅkāra*).

*Ahaṅkāra* is a real *prakṛti*-tattva that the *jīva* is associated with under *karma*; it is one of the contracting conditions of *dharmabhūta-jñāna*. At *mokṣa* the *jīva* is freed from *prākṛta-ahaṅkāra* but its individual self-reference (*svayaṃ-jñāna*) persists eternally.

*Ahaṅkāra* is a real *prakṛti*-tattva. Crucially, the *jīva*'s self-reference is NOT itself *ahaṅkāra* in the pejorative sense — Madhva insists on the eternal individuality of the *jīva*; what binds is *prākṛta*-ahaṅkāra (matter-tinged ego), not the *jīva*'s ineliminable self-reference.

translator_note: 'Ego' is the standard gloss but imports Freudian and modern-Western connotations. *Ahaṅkāra* is more precisely 'the I-making function' — a technical psychological-metaphysical category, not Freud's drive-balancing apparatus. The user's position-brief notes that *ahaṅkāra* is what blocks the divine 'in true form' (T6, T8); 'ego' captures only the affective-attachment side, missing the cognitive-fabrication side.
